sections.question_synthetic: The writer is requesting reconsideration of FPPC Advice Letter No. I-92-351, arguing that their situation qualifies for the 'protocol' exception to the definition of 'gift' under the Political Reform Act because the facts are analogous to those in the White letter, contrary to the FPPC's conclusion.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming correspondence challenging prior FPPC advice, not an FPPC response letter. No formal Q/A sections present. The document references prior Advice Letter I-92-351 and disputes the FPPC's factual distinctions. Written by outside counsel (Nielsen, Merksamer law firm) on behalf of an unidentified client.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: The writer is requesting reconsideration of FPPC Advice Letter No. I-92-351, arguing that their situation qualifies for the 'protocol' exception to the definition of 'gift' under the Political Reform Act because the facts are analogous to those in the White letter, contrary to the FPPC's conclusion.

embedding.summary: This is a follow-up correspondence from an attorney challenging the FPPC's previous advice letter (I-92-351) regarding whether a proposed reception situation qualifies for the protocol exception to gift rules, arguing that the facts are substantially similar to the White letter precedent cited by the FPPC.
